data.title: Circle is about to launch a packaged Bitcoin cirBTC
data.description: Stablecoin issuer Circle announced on its X platform the upcoming launch of cirBTC, a Bitcoin-encapsulated token. cirBTC is backed 1:1 by BTC and is verifiable on-chain. It is designed to work seamlessly with Circle's infrastructure and the wider DeFi ecosystem, creating a neutral infrastructure for new on-chain applications.

data.title: Circle to Launch Bitcoin-Backed cirBTC for Seamless DeFi Integration
data.description: Circle announced on April 3 the introduction of cirBTC, a Bitcoin-backed token designed for seamless integration with Circle's infrastructure and the broader DeFi ecosystem. According to BlockBeats, cirBTC will be supported by Bitcoin on a 1:1 basis and can be verified on-chain at any time. The initiative aims to enhance interoperability within decentralized finance platforms.

data.title: European Central Bank Likely to Raise Key Interest Rates in Next Adjustment
data.description: The European Central Bank is expected to increase key interest rates in its upcoming adjustment. According to ChainCatcher, ECB Governing Council member Villeroy stated that the next rate change is highly likely to be an upward adjustment.

data.title: ECB's Villeroy: Premature to Predict Rate Hike Timeline, Ready to Act When Necessary
data.description: European Central Bank (ECB) Governing Council member François Villeroy de Galhau stated that it is too early to forecast the timeline for the ECB's interest rate hikes. According to Jin10, Villeroy emphasized the ECB's readiness to take action whenever necessary, regardless of timing or method. His comments reflect the ongoing uncertainty in the economic landscape and the ECB's commitment to responding to evolving conditions.

data.title: Sky Protocol Introduces Sky Agent Network for USDS Liquidity Allocation
data.description: Sky Protocol has launched the Sky Agent Network, providing a framework for independent capital allocators to borrow USDS for diversified strategy allocation. According to NS3.AI, the Sky Ecosystem announced that the initial group of allocators includes Securitize, Maple Finance, and Centrifuge. This model allows multiple operators to compete for USDS liquidity, moving away from reliance on a single allocation channel.

data.title: Italy to Receive LNG from Qatar's Golden Pass Facility Starting June, Sources Say
data.description: Italy is set to begin receiving liquefied natural gas (LNG) from the Golden Pass LNG facility, a joint venture between Qatar Energy and ExxonMobil, starting in June. According to Jin10, this move aims to help Italy address supply shortages from the Gulf region. The Golden Pass facility is expected to play a crucial role in ensuring energy security for Italy by providing a stable supply of LNG.

data.title: Yesterday, the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net outflow of 2,254 BTC, and the Ethereum ETF saw a net outflow of 17,197 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, yesterday the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net outflow of 2,254 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net outflow of 17,197 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 1,531 SOL.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Nvidia and Google Collaborate to Optimize Gemma 4 for NVIDIA GPUs
data.description: Nvidia and Google have announced a collaboration to optimize Gemma 4 for use with NVIDIA GPUs. According to Jin10, this partnership aims to enhance the performance and efficiency of Gemma 4, leveraging Nvidia's advanced GPU technology. The optimization is expected to improve processing capabilities and expand the application of Gemma 4 in various sectors. This move highlights the ongoing efforts by major tech companies to advance AI technologies and integrate them with powerful hardware solutions.

data.title: Kroll assigns Ripple Prime a “Medium Quality” BBB rating, the lowest investment level.
data.description: Kroll Credit Rating Agency (KBRA) has rated Ripple Prime's U.S. broker-dealer and holding company as "Medium Quality" (BBB), the lowest investment grade. Ripple Prime provides integrated trading, lending, and settlement services to institutional investors. KBRA noted that the rating reflects its "expansion phase," with revenue primarily concentrated in exchange-traded derivatives clearing and short-term Treasury financing. (The Block)

data.title: D1 Capital Partners Invests in Amazon, Spotify, and Autodesk in Q4 2025
data.description: D1 Capital Partners, led by Daniel Sundheim, made significant investments in Amazon, Spotify, and Autodesk during the fourth quarter of 2025, totaling $674.201 million. According to NS3.AI, the hedge fund also completely exited nine positions in the same period, including Primo Brands, Nu Holdings, and Carpenter Technologies.

data.title: Expert Highlights DeFi's Focus on Gas Efficiency Over Market Resilience
data.description: Joao Garcia has raised concerns about the current structure of decentralized finance (DeFi), emphasizing its focus on gas-efficient execution rather than market resilience. According to NS3.AI, Garcia pointed out that many DeFi protocols depend on static risk models, which may not be sufficient during periods of market stress. He suggested that enhanced onchain computing capabilities could enable these protocols to conduct stress testing and risk recalculations with greater transparency.

data.title: Severe Weather in Greece Causes Fatalities and Infrastructure Damage
data.description:
Greece has been hit by severe convective weather for two consecutive days, resulting in at least one death and significant flooding and infrastructure damage. According to RTHK, heavy rain and strong winds have affected various parts of Greece since last Wednesday, with the Attica region, where Athens is located, being particularly hard-hit. The fire department reported approximately 500 emergency calls in Attica alone, mainly concerning flooded homes, fallen trees, and people trapped in vehicles. A man in his 50s was swept away by floodwaters and died.

In the southern regions of Rhodes and Poros, the storm caused river overflows, road damage, and power outages. Winds reached force 9, severely impacting maritime traffic, with many ships forced to halt operations and coastal shipping disrupted. The meteorological department forecasts that the extreme weather will persist, potentially continuing in northern Greece until the 4th of this month. Authorities have urged residents to minimize outdoor activities, stay safe, and be cautious of secondary disasters.

data.title: The US CTFC and the Department of Justice have sued the governor and attorney general of Illinois, alleging that they lacked the authority to regulate the prediction market.
data.description: According to market sources, the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and the Department of Justice (DOJ) have filed a lawsuit against the governor and attorney general of Illinois, accusing them of exceeding their legal authority in attempting to regulate prediction markets. The two agencies argue that Illinois' regulatory measures are invalid because the state government has no jurisdiction over federally regulated prediction markets. The lawsuit emphasizes the need to protect innovative financial instruments from unauthorized interference by local governments. (Reuters)

data.title: Coinbase says it has received conditional approval for a U.S. trust license.
data.description: According to market sources, Coinbase has announced conditional approval for a US trust license, marking another step towards offering digital asset services such as custody under federal regulation. If finalized, this license will help strengthen its compliance capabilities for institutional clients.

data.title: Bitcoin mining company Riot Platforms has transferred another 500 BTC, worth $33.26 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Bitcoin mining company Riot Platforms has transferred another 500 BTC, worth $33.26 million, suggesting a possible sale.

data.title: CFTC and DOJ File Lawsuit Against Illinois Over Prediction Market Regulation
data.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and the Department of Justice (DOJ) have filed a lawsuit against the state of Illinois, its governor, and attorney general. According to BlockBeats, the lawsuit alleges that Illinois is attempting to regulate prediction markets beyond its legal authority, rendering such actions invalid. The case highlights ongoing tensions between federal and state jurisdictions over the regulation of emerging financial markets.

data.title: Iranian crude oil prices are trading at a premium to Brent crude for the first time since 2022.
data.description: According to Argus Media data, Iranian crude oil has traded at a premium to the global benchmark Brent crude for the first time since May 2022, following the temporary easing of some US sanctions on Iranian oil. On March 26, Iranian main export-grade crude was trading at a premium of approximately $1 per barrel to Brent, compared to a discount of approximately $10 per barrel earlier this year under comprehensive sanctions. This change indicates that Tehran is benefiting from its control of the Strait of Hormuz. With Brent crude holding at around $107 per barrel and global supply constrained, buyers are willing to pay a premium for crude that remains available. By blocking passage for Gulf oil-producing countries while allowing its own cargo to pass, Iran has created a transportation bottleneck, driving up global oil prices and increasing the relative value of Iranian light crude. Tanker tracking data shows that vessels associated with Iran are still transporting goods through strategic waterways and unloading from floating storage facilities. (Jinshi)

data.title: WTI Crude Drops Nearly $5 Amid Strait of Hormuz Transit Agreement Talks
data.description: WTI crude oil prices experienced a significant decline, dropping nearly $5 to $108.93 per barrel. According to NS3.AI, this sharp short-term movement comes amid reports that Iran is in the process of drafting a transit agreement with Oman concerning the Strait of Hormuz. The potential agreement could have implications for oil transportation through this critical maritime passage.

data.title: Ripple Prime Receives Investment Grade Rating from Kroll
data.description: Global credit rating agency Kroll has awarded Ripple's brokerage platform, Ripple Prime, an investment-grade issuer rating of BBB. According to Foresight News, Kroll stated that the issuer rating for Ripple Prime US indicates that its business model is in the scaling phase. The focus is primarily on clearing and intermediary services within its exchange-traded derivatives (ETD) platform, set to launch in 2024, as well as similar activities in the fixed income repo sector. These activities are expected to reach significant scale by 2025, concentrating mainly on short-term U.S. Treasury and agency securities.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Vitalik Buterin Explores Secure AI Personal Use Solutions
data.description:
Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has detailed his exploration of localized, private, and secure AI personal use solutions. According to PANews, Buterin highlighted significant privacy and security concerns within the current AI landscape, including local open-source AI. He pointed out vulnerabilities such as OpenClaw agents modifying critical settings without human confirmation and malicious external inputs easily taking over user instances. Some skills even contain harmful instructions.

Buterin advocates for prioritizing local inference and sandbox isolation for all large language models (LLMs) and documents. He conducted tests using hardware like the NVIDIA 5090 laptop and AMD Ryzen AI Max Pro, employing the Qwen3.5:35B model through llama-server and utilizing the NixOS system.
